claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'd like to help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me start by asking: **How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you! And can you confirm: **Does Alex live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me here in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! That's helpful information. Now I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you responsible for Alex's care? For example, do you provide for his needs such as food, clothes, and other day-to-day expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all of his care - his food, clothes, school things, everything he needs day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. One final question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or has he been for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in hospital or care or anything like that. He lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 12 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for his care and provide for all his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He is not in hospital or care

You meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, you can still claim Child Benefit - you would just need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

If you'd like to proceed with your claim, you can apply for Child Benefit through the official government channels. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement for Child Benefit. He lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for his care, providing for all his day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and school items. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation - he lives at home full-time. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and no one else is claiming for the same child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 12), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and provides for all his needs. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

He lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for his care, providing for all his day-to-day needs including food, clothes, and school items. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation - he lives at home full-time. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and no one else is claiming for the same child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 12), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. The claimant is fully responsible for Alex's care and provides for all his needs. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and he is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
